title: Cistanthe longiscapa
taxonomy: Cistanthe longiscapa is classified within the family Portulacaceae. The genus Cistanthe is a group of flowering plants commonly known as "mattresses" or "bladders" due to the shape of their seed capsules. Cistanthe longiscapa is a species of flowering plant native to South America. The classification of Cistanthe longiscapa has undergone changes over time, with some sources placing it in the family Montiaceae. intro: Cistanthe longiscapa, also known as Chilean Pink or Pink Rock-pink, is a flowering plant species in the family Portulacaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Cistanthe longiscapa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
